If a target has already received damage during the same turn that Assurance is used, the move will deal double damage. This effect includes indirect, self-inflicted, recoil, or crash damage, as well as damage from items like a Life Orb.
What is the primary effect of the move Embargo on a target Pokémon?

For five turns, the target's held item has its effects negated, and its Trainer cannot use items from the Bag on it. This effect ends if the Pokémon switches out. Additionally, an affected Pokémon cannot use Fling or Natural Gift.
What is the primary effect of holding a Douse Drive on a Genesect's Techno Blast move?
When a Genesect holds the Douse Drive, its Techno Blast move changes to the Water type. Additionally, holding this item causes the Genesect to change into its Douse Drive form.
At what level does a Ponyta evolve into Rapidash?
Ponyta evolves into Rapidash at level 40.
What is the primary typing and base stat distribution for the Mythical Pokémon Deoxys?
Deoxys is a Psychic-type Mythical Pokémon. Its base stats are 50 HP, 150 Attack, 50 Defense, 150 Special Attack, 50 Special Defense, and 150 Speed.
How should I play Solgaleo in competitive matches according to the strategy guide?

Solgaleo functions as a defensive wall, particularly against Eternatus, Ultra Necrozma, Desolate Land Mega Blaziken, and non-Headlong Rush Kyurem-B. Key moves include Psychic Noise to prevent healing, Flip Turn for chip damage, Teleport for pivoting, Gigaton Hammer to pressure Fairy-types, and Spikes to reduce passivity. Depending on the build, an Impish nature is used for bulk, while Jolly outruns Mega Blaziken and Mega Gardevoir.
What happens if a Pokémon uses the move Copycat?

When a Pokémon uses Copycat, it mimics the move that was used successfully immediately before it by any Pokémon. The move will fail if no other move has been used yet, and it cannot copy itself, roar, or whirlwind. Additionally, Copycat cannot be selected by assist, metronome, or sleep talk, nor can it be copied by mirror move.
What is the primary effect of holding a Waterium Z in battle?
The Waterium Z is a held item that allows a Pokémon to upgrade its Water-type moves into their corresponding Z-move equivalents using crystallized Z-Power.
What are the base stats and primary abilities of the Pokemon Yanmega?
Yanmega has the following base stats: HP 86, Attack 76, Defense 86, Sp. Atk 116, Sp. Def 56, and Speed 95. Its available abilities are Speed Boost and Tinted Lens, while its hidden ability is Frisk.
What is the primary effect of the move Psycho Cut regarding critical hits?

Psycho Cut is a move that inflicts regular damage and features a high critical-hit ratio. When using this move, the user's critical hit rate is increased by one level, making critical hits more likely to land.
What is the primary role and strategy for Guts Conkeldurr in the UU tier?

Guts Conkeldurr serves as a devastating wallbreaker in the UU tier. By holding a Flame Orb, it triggers its Guts ability to boost its power and becomes immune to other status moves. It uses coverage moves like Knock Off, Ice Punch, or Facade alongside powerful STAB options such as Drain Punch or Close Combat to dismantle defensive cores. Mach Punch provides essential priority to threaten faster foes like Excadrill and Hydreigon, though its Flame Orb and low Speed make it susceptible to being worn down.
Why is Gothitelle considered ineffective in the BW OU metagame, and which other Psychic-types are recommended instead?

Gothitelle is ineffective because the ban of Shadow Tag stripped away its niche as a trapper, and its mediocre stats make it outclassed by other Psychic-types. It is recommended to use Latios, Alakazam, Reuniclus, Jirachi, Latias, Mew, or Victini instead, as they provide superior damage output and team support.
What is the primary role of Iron Treads in competitive play, and what items can it hold to improve its performance?

Iron Treads serves as an excellent entry hazard control option, utilizing Stealth Rock and Rapid Spin to manage hazards while checking Pokemon like Raging Bolt and Gholdengo. To improve its performance, Leftovers increases longevity; Air Balloon allows it to switch into Ground-type attacks from foes like Gliscor or Landorus-T; Clear Amulet blocks Intimidate and Parting Shot; and Assault Vest provides special bulk when Stealth Rock support is not needed.
What is the primary function of the item known as Data Card 11?
Data Card 11 is used to record the number of points a trainer has earned and display the scores achieved by their Pokémon in the Pokéathlon.
What happens when a Pokémon with the Aftermath ability is knocked out by a move that makes contact?
When a Pokémon with the Aftermath ability is knocked out by a move that makes contact, the user of that move takes damage equal to 1/4 of their maximum HP.
What happens when you use a Bold Mint on a Pokémon?

When a Bold Mint is used from the Bag, it changes the effect of a Pokémon's Nature on its stats to that of the Bold Nature, which results in an increase to its Defense stat and a decrease to its Attack stat.
What is the primary role of Vanilluxe in XY competitive play and how does its Weak Armor ability assist it?

Vanilluxe serves as an excellent wallbreaker due to its offensive power, solid Speed, and offensive typing. Its ability, Weak Armor, grants it a situational Speed boost when it is hit by contact moves like Knock Off or U-turn, which can be advantageous in late-game scenarios against attackers such as Persian.
At what levels does Chikorita evolve into its subsequent forms?
Chikorita evolves into Bayleef at level 16, and Bayleef evolves into Meganium at level 32.
How is the RU tier defined in terms of Pokémon usage and availability?

RU is Smogon's third usage-based tier, comprising Pokémon that are not OU, UU, Ubers, UUBL, or RUBL. A Pokémon is designated 'RU' if it makes up at least ~3.41% of total weighted usage in the tier, which means a typical player is more than 50% likely to encounter that Pokémon at least once in 20 battles.
What are the specific effects and turn requirements of the move Sky Attack?

Sky Attack is a two-turn move where the user charges on the first turn and attacks on the second turn. It inflicts regular damage, has a higher critical hit chance than normal, and may cause the target to flinch. Additionally, this move cannot be selected by sleep talk.
What is the primary function of the Shaderoot Carrot in Generation VIII?
The Shaderoot Carrot is a Key Item introduced in Generation VIII that allows the player to summon the Legendary Pokémon Spectrier at the Crown Shrine.
What is the Pickup ability and how does it function during and after a battle?

Pickup allows a Pokémon to retrieve an item if another Pokémon consumed or flung one during a turn. After a battle, the Pokémon has a 10% chance to find an item if it is not currently holding one. Items cannot be picked up if the Pokémon is already holding an item, and specific items like the air balloon and eject button are excluded from this ability.
What effect does the Earth Plate have on a Pokémon's moves and the move Judgment?
The Earth Plate boosts the power of the holder's Ground-type moves by 20%. Additionally, if the holder uses the move Judgment, it becomes a Ground-type move.
What happens when a Pokémon uses the move Uproar?

When a Pokémon uses Uproar, it inflicts regular damage and is forced to repeat the move for 2 to 5 turns. During this effect, all Pokémon on the field wake up and are prevented from falling asleep, and the move Rest cannot be used. Additionally, Uproar cannot be selected by the move Sleep Talk.
What is Musharna's role in the PU tier and what are its key strengths?

In the PU tier, Musharna acts as a reliable answer to Fighting-types like Gurdurr and Hitmonchan due to its bulk and Moonlight recovery. It is the only viable Future Sight user, providing wallbreaking support, and can run Healing Wish to support sweepers. Additionally, it can function as a late-game sweeper or stallbreaker using Calm Mind and Barrier sets, though it is limited by low Speed, low Moonlight PP, and weaknesses to Dark-type Pursuit users like Absol and Skuntank.
Why is Drednaw generally considered an inferior entry hazard lead compared to Kabutops and Crustle?

Drednaw is outclassed because it lacks Kabutops's utility moves like Knock Off and Rapid Spin, which help manage hazards, and it lacks Crustle's Sturdy ability, which provides late-game flexibility. While Drednaw can use Jaw Lock to trap specific hazard setters like Stunfisk and Clefairy, its inability to match the specific utility and speed benchmarks of its competitors makes it less effective.
What is Krookodile's role in the UU tier and what key moves help it succeed?

In the UU tier, Krookodile serves as a decent Stealth Rock setter, utilizing its strong offensive potential and utility options. Its effectiveness is bolstered by moves like Earthquake and Knock Off, which hit many targets hard, while Stealth Rock and Taunt allow it to manage Defoggers like Mandibuzz and Moltres. Additionally, the ability Intimidate helps it switch into physical attacks, and Toxic can be used to cripple checks such as Tangrowth and Tapu Bulu.
What Pokémon does the Claw Fossil regenerate into, and how has its classification changed since its introduction?

The Claw Fossil can be regenerated into an Anorith. Originally introduced in Generation III as a Key Item, it was reclassified as a normal item starting in Generation IV. This change allowed players to hold multiple fossils, carry them as held items, and trade them.
What is the recommended item and moveset for a Swords Dance Pangoro in SM competitive play?

The recommended moveset includes Swords Dance, Knock Off, Drain Punch, and Bullet Punch. Chople Berry is the preferred item to survive a Close Combat from Passimian, though Life Orb is an alternative for more power. The build utilizes the Iron Fist ability, an Adamant nature, and maximum Attack and Speed EVs to maximize its role as a wallbreaker and late-game sweeper.
What effect does the Zap Plate have on a Pokémon holding it?
The Zap Plate increases the power of the holder's Electric-type moves by 20%. Additionally, if held by a Pokémon with the Multitype ability, the holder's type becomes Electric.
What is the competitive viability of Cinccino in the NU tier?

Cinccino is considered a horribly outclassed Pokemon in the NU tier. It struggles to break through physically defensive Pokemon like Steelix, Weezing, and Ferroseed due to its middling power and lack of options to hit Steel-types super effectively. While it can use a Choice Band set with Skill Link to pressure offensive teams and special walls, it is generally outperformed by other wallbreakers like Sneasel, Heliolisk, and Aerodactyl.
What is the primary role of Swords Dance Rillaboom and which teammates help it overcome its struggle against Fire-types?

Swords Dance Rillaboom functions as a late-game cleaner and a dangerous revenge killer against foes like Darkrai, Iron Valiant, and Ogerpon-W. It often struggles against Fire-types like Iron Moth, Heatran, and Moltres, making Water-types that can threaten them, such as Ogerpon-W and Primarina, excellent teammates. Primarina is especially valued as it also offers pivot support.
At what levels does Bulbasaur evolve into Ivysaur and subsequently into Venusaur?
Bulbasaur evolves into Ivysaur at level 16, and Ivysaur evolves into Venusaur at level 32.
How does the power of the move Wring Out work?
Wring Out is a Normal-type move that deals damage based on the target's current HP. The power of the move is greater the higher the enemy's current HP is, with power varying between 1 and 120.
Why is Heatmor considered an inferior choice compared to other Fire-types like Rapidash and Ninetales in the SS metagame?

Heatmor is considered outclassed because it is slower and frailer than Rapidash and Ninetales. Furthermore, those Fire-types benefit from superior bulk, speed, access to more reliable boosting options, and better coverage moves, making them more effective choices for a team.
At what level does Zubat evolve into Golbat?
Zubat evolves into Golbat starting at level 22.
How does the Mirror Herb item function when held by a Pokémon?

When an opponent increases their stats, the holder of the Mirror Herb consumes the item to copy those exact same stat increases. Additionally, if a Pokémon holds a Mirror Herb and has an empty move slot during a picnic, it can learn an Egg Move from another Pokémon in the party that knows it, regardless of Egg Group or gender; this process does not consume the item.
What are the effects of the move Petal Dance when used in battle?

Petal Dance inflicts regular damage and forces the user to attack for 2 to 3 turns. Once the final hit concludes, the user becomes confused. Notably, the move Safeguard does not protect the user from this confusion.
How does the move Terrain Pulse function during battle?
Terrain Pulse inflicts regular damage by utilizing the power of the terrain. Its type and power change based on the specific terrain present at the time the move is used.
What happens to a Pokémon affected by the move Nightmare?
If a sleeping Pokémon is hit by Nightmare, it will take damage equal to 1/4 of its maximum HP every turn. This effect concludes if the target wakes up or leaves the battle field.
What happens when a Pokémon uses Focus Punch if it takes damage during the same turn?
If the user of Focus Punch takes damage during the turn before it executes the move, the move will fail.
What is the primary effect of the Pokémon move Spectral Thief?
Spectral Thief steals the target's stat increases and then deals regular damage. It will not raise any of the user's stats beyond +6, leaving any excess increases on the target.
What are the base stats and abilities of the Water-type Pokémon Tympole?
Tympole has a base HP of 50, Attack of 50, Defense of 40, Sp. Atk of 50, Sp. Def of 40, and Speed of 64. Its primary abilities are Swift Swim and Hydration, and its hidden ability is Water Absorb.
Which specific Pokemon can Crustle deal significant damage to according to its BW competitive strategy?
In its BW competitive strategy, Crustle is noted for being able to deal significant damage to Slowking, Tangrowth, Uxie, Golurk, Qwilfish, and Steelix.
What is the primary role of Cofagrigus in its IronPress set and which teammates support it effectively?

Cofagrigus functions as a defensive IronPress sweeper that excels against physical attackers like Sawk and Gurdurr. It fits best on bulkier builds and appreciates Dark-resistant teammates such as Skuntank, Silvally-Fighting, or Alcremie, which can provide status support via Aromatherapy. Additionally, it benefits from special walls like Articuno or Clefairy and Spikes support from Pokémon like Qwilfish or Coalossal to prevent opponents from pivoting around it.
What requirements must be met for Nincada to evolve into Shedinja?

To evolve Nincada into Shedinja, the player must have an empty slot in their party and an extra regular Poké Ball on hand when Nincada evolves into Ninjask at level 20. The extra Poké Ball is consumed during the process, though this requirement was not necessary in Generation III.
What is unique about the Ability possessed by Archeops and its pre-evolved form, Archen?
Archeops and its pre-evolved form, Archen, are the only known Pokémon that can have the Ability called Defeatist.
What are the effects of the Ice-type move Chilly Reception?

Chilly Reception is a non-damaging move that creates snow on the battlefield for five turns, or eight turns if the user holds an Icy Rock, and switches the user out. If there are no teammates available to switch to, it will still change the weather to snow. The user will switch out even if snow is already active on the field.
What were the primary uses of Armaldo's physical features when it lived in ancient times?

In ancient times, Armaldo used the large wings on its back for swimming and its thick shell to protect itself from enemy attacks. Additionally, its strong claws allowed it to break stones, punch through steel slabs, and capture prey.
What is the function of the Pokémon item TR59?
TR59 is a Pokémon item that allows the user to slam a barrage of hard-shelled seeds down on the target from above.
What is the primary effect of the ability Bulletproof?
The ability Bulletproof protects the Pokémon from bullet, ball, and bomb-based moves.
What is the primary function of the Camping Gear item in the Generation VIII Pokémon games?

In Generation VIII, the Camping Gear is a Key Item that allows the player to set up a Pokémon Camp. It cannot be used in certain locations like buildings or on water, nor can it be used during specific challenges such as Gym battles, the Champion Cup tournament, or the Galarian Star Tournament.
What roles does Excadrill fill in the UU tier and what are its primary checks?

In UU, Excadrill acts as an Electric-immune entry hazard remover, Stealth Rock setter, and late-game cleaner. It checks foes like Nihilego, Togekiss, Jirachi, and Galarian Slowbro. However, it is vulnerable to Water-, Grass-, and Fighting-type Pokemon such as Keldeo, Zarude, and Conkeldurr, which outspeed or use priority against it. It also struggles to bypass Skarmory, Hippowdon, Moltres, and Celesteela.
What is the recommended item for Eldegoss in competitive play and what role does it serve on a team?

Heavy-Duty Boots is the best item for Eldegoss because it frequently switches in to sponge damage. Eldegoss serves as a top-tier Rapid Spin user that can status Ghost-types like Doublade and Jellicent, acts as a defensive pivot due to its Regenerator ability, and effectively checks Ground-, Water-, and Rock-types such as Sandaconda, Jellicent, and Regirock.
What are the primary vulnerabilities and counters for Meloetta in competitive play?

Meloetta struggles against Steel-types such as Klefki, Bronzong, and Copperajah, which wall its primary STAB moves. It is also outsped by revenge killers like Paldean Tauros-W, Flygon, and Cinccino. Additionally, Scream Tail can neutralize Meloetta's setup sets with fast Encore, and Meloetta faces stiff competition from other special attackers like Toxtricity, Munkidori, and Porygon-Z, which provide better immediate power or speed control.
What is the recommended role, item, and ability for Leavanny in the PU tier?

Leavanny is used as a suicide lead in the PU tier to set up Sticky Web. The preferred item is Focus Sash, which ensures the move is set up at least once and activates the preferred ability, Swarm, to boost X-Scissor damage after the item is consumed.
How should Aegislash be built to effectively check Geomancy Xerneas in XY competitive play?

To check Geomancy Xerneas, Aegislash should be invested with maximum HP and Special Defense, a Sassy nature, and 0 Speed IVs to maximize the damage output of Gyro Ball. It should hold Leftovers for reliable recovery and utilize King's Shield to scout moves, drop the opponent's Attack on contact, and regain defensive stats. Toxic is used to punish switch-ins, while Gyro Ball serves as its strongest offensive move against Xerneas.
What are the base stats and primary abilities of the Rock-type Pokémon Bonsly?
Bonsly has a base HP of 50, Attack of 80, Defense of 95, Sp. Atk of 10, Sp. Def of 45, and Speed of 10. Its primary abilities are Sturdy and Rock Head, and its hidden ability is Rattled.
What is the recommended EV spread for a defensive Brambleghast in the NU tier?

For defensive utility sets, Brambleghast should run an EV spread of 252 HP / 80 Def / 176 Spe with a Jolly nature, which helps it better handle physical wallbreakers like Flygon, Breloom, and Paldean Tauros-W.
What is the primary effect of the Shadow Tag ability in battle?

The Shadow Tag ability prevents opposing Pokémon from fleeing or switching out during battle. However, this effect does not apply to other Pokémon that also possess Shadow Tag, Pokémon with the Run Away ability, or instances where a Pokémon switches out using a specific move or item.
What is Turtonator's role in the PU metagame and what is its primary strategy?

Turtonator serves as a threatening Shell Smash sweeper in the PU metagame. Its primary strategy involves using Shell Smash to boost its stats, allowing it to sweep weakened teams or break down specially defensive walls. It often utilizes Draco Meteor paired with Dragonium Z for high power or White Herb to mitigate the defensive drops from Shell Smash, taking advantage of its Fire/Dragon typing to find setup opportunities against certain Fire, Steel, and Electric-type moves.
At what level does a Galarian Ponyta evolve into a Galarian Rapidash?
A Galarian Ponyta evolves into a Galarian Rapidash starting at level 40.
At what levels does Pidgeotto evolve from and into its other forms?
Pidgeotto evolves from Pidgey starting at level 18 and evolves into Pidgeot starting at level 36.
What is a notable characteristic of the Karate Chop move in Pokémon?
Karate Chop is a move that inflicts regular damage and features a high critical hit ratio, meaning the user's critical hit rate is one level higher when using it.
What is the evolution method for Honedge?
Honedge evolves into Doublade starting at level 35, and Doublade then evolves into Aegislash when exposed to a Dusk Stone.
What is the role of Dragapult in the SV OU tier and why is Infiltrator the preferred ability?

Dragapult serves a unique role in SV OU as the fastest user of status moves and U-turn in the tier, allowing it to pivot and maintain momentum. Its preferred ability is Infiltrator, which enables it to hit and cripple foes even if they are protected by Substitute or screens from Pokemon like Kyurem or Galarian Moltres.
What is the specific effect of holding a Ganlon Berry in battle?
When a Pokémon holding a Ganlon Berry has 1/4 of its maximum HP remaining or less, it consumes the berry to raise its Defense stat by one stage.
What is the primary role and strategy for a Guts Conkeldurr in BW competitive play?

Conkeldurr utilizes the Guts ability alongside the Bulk Up move to function as a status-absorbing tank. By boosting its Attack and Defense, it becomes a powerful offensive force capable of setting up against Pokemon like Tyranitar and Ferrothorn. It relies on Drain Punch for STAB damage and recovery, Mach Punch for priority, and Payback to hit Ghost- and Psychic-types, often taking advantage of its low Speed to land higher-damage hits.
Why is Maractus considered an unviable choice in the SS ZU tier?

Maractus is unviable in SS ZU because it is outclassed as a defensive Spikes setter by Roselia. Roselia possesses superior bulk, especially when using Eviolite, and offers better offensive and defensive typing. Consequently, Maractus struggles to support its team or counter top Pokemon effectively compared to Roselia.
What are the base stats and primary ability of the Electric/Ghost type Pokémon, Rotom?
Rotom has base stats of 50 HP, 50 Attack, 77 Defense, 95 Sp. Atk, 77 Sp. Def, and 91 Speed. Its primary ability is Levitate.
What happens to the user's held item when the move Fling is used?
When the move Fling is used, the user's held item is consumed. The item is flung at the target to attack, and the move's power and effects depend on that specific held item.
What happens to a target's stat modifiers when hit by the move Clear Smog?
When the move Clear Smog is used, it inflicts regular damage and resets all of the target's stat modifiers to zero.
What is the effect of holding the Rock Memory item?

The Rock Memory is a memory disc containing Rock-type data. When held by a Silvally, it changes the Pokémon into its Rock form and changes the type of Multi-Attack to Rock. For certain species of Pokémon, it changes the type of the holder.
What is the recommended role for Toucannon in competitive play, and how effective is it?

Toucannon serves as a niche wallbreaker and late-game cleaner, but it is generally ineffective. Due to poor bulk and a disappointing Speed tier, it struggles to utilize its high Attack and utility moves effectively. It is considered a final-round draft pick that should only be selected if the team is already self-sufficient, as it will likely see very little playtime.
At what level does a Wynaut evolve into Wobbuffet, and what are its primary abilities?
Wynaut evolves into Wobbuffet at level 15. Its primary ability is Shadow Tag, and its hidden ability is Telepathy.
How do Scrafty groups determine their hierarchy?

Scrafty groups determine their hierarchy based on the size of the Pokémon's crest. The Scrafty that possesses the largest crest becomes the leader of the group, and the size of the crest also influences how arrogantly the individual Scrafty acts.
What are the different things referred to by the name Metronome in the Pokémon franchise?
The name Metronome refers to three distinct things in the Pokémon franchise: a move introduced in Generation I, a held item in the Pokémon games, and an application for the e-Reader.
What are the base stats and primary typing of Garchomp?
Garchomp is a Dragon/Ground type Pokémon with the following base stats: HP 108, Attack 130, Defense 95, Sp. Atk 80, Sp. Def 85, and Speed 102.
What is the recommended moveset and item for Noivern in SS, and what role does it play?

Noivern functions as an offensive pivot or Defogger, utilizing Heavy-Duty Boots to avoid Stealth Rock damage. Its moveset includes U-turn to provide free switches, Draco Meteor to threaten Pokemon like Flygon and Xurkitree, and Flamethrower to hit Steel-types like Steelix and Metagross. Defog can be chosen for hazard removal. The Infiltrator ability allows it to ignore Substitute from foes like Raikou and Chandelure.
What is the secondary effect of the move Armor Cannon?
When the user performs the move Armor Cannon, it fires its armor as blazing projectiles, which subsequently lowers the user's Defense and Sp. Def stats.
What is the primary effect of the move Roar when used against a target?

When Roar is used, it forces the target to switch out for another Pokémon from its trainer’s party selected at random. If used in a wild battle against a single Pokémon, the battle ends immediately. The move does not affect Pokémon that have the Suction Cups ability or are currently under the effect of Ingrain.
What is the recommended EV spread and nature for a Choice Scarf Gourgeist in the PU metagame?

To function effectively with a Choice Scarf in the PU metagame, Gourgeist should run 252 EVs in Attack with an Adamant nature to maximize damage output. It should also have 212 EVs in Speed, which allows it to outspeed the entire unboosted metagame and all Pokemon up to +1 Fraxure, with the remaining EVs allocated to HP to increase its bulk.
What is unique about the move Overdrive in terms of its type and category?
Overdrive is an Electric-type move that is also sound-based. It holds the distinction of being the only Electric-type sound-based move in the game.
What items does Corviknight commonly use, and why is its typing advantageous for its role as a Defogger?

Corviknight uses items like Leftovers or Rocky Helmet because its typing provides a neutrality to Stealth Rock, making it unnecessary to run Heavy-Duty Boots. Its defensive typing is a key component of its success as a Defogger and defensive pivot.
What is the primary effect of the move Brine when used against a target?
Brine is a move taught by TM55 that shoots boiling hot water at a target. If the target's HP is half or less, the attack hits with double the power.
How does the ability Illusion function when a Pokémon enters a battle?

When a Pokémon with Illusion enters battle, it appears as the species, nickname, and Poké Ball of the last Pokémon in the Trainer's party that is able to battle. This disguise remains in effect until the Pokémon is hit by a damaging move, though damage from sources like weather, spikes, or attacks against a substitute will not break the illusion.
What are the common checks and counters to Samurott in the XY competitive metagame?

Samurott is checked by various common Grass- and Water-types, including Vileplume, Ludicolo, Lanturn, and Poliwrath. Additionally, it is countered by physically bulky Pokemon like Weezing and Garbodor if it is using a physical set, and by specially bulky Pokemon like Hariyama and Mega Audino if it is running a special set.
What is the evolution level for a Malamar and what are its base stats?
Malamar evolves from Inkay at level 30. Its base stats are as follows: HP 86, Attack 92, Defense 88, Sp. Atk 68, Sp. Def 75, and Speed 73.
What are the specific requirements to obtain a Shedinja when Nincada evolves?
To obtain a Shedinja, the player must have an empty slot in their party and an extra Poké Ball on hand when Nincada evolves into Ninjask starting at level 20.
What is the primary effect of using a Silver Razz Berry in Pokémon?
The Silver Razz Berry is a Pokémon item that makes it easier to catch Pokémon when it is given to them.
How does the Friend Guard Ability affect the damage taken by allied Pokémon in battle?

Friend Guard reduces the damage received by other allied Pokémon from attacks by 25%. It does not reduce damage dealt to the Pokémon with the Ability itself, nor does it affect moves that deal direct damage. If multiple allies have Friend Guard, the effects stack multiplicatively.
What happens when a Pokémon uses the move Bulk Up?
When a Pokémon uses the move Bulk Up, it tenses its muscles to bulk up its body, which raises both the user's Attack and Defense stats by one stage each.
What is the primary role of Choice Scarf Hoopa on an offensive team, and which partners help compensate for its vulnerability to Dark-types?

Choice Scarf Hoopa acts as both a form of speed control and a fast wallbreaker, capable of overwhelming both offensive and defensive foes. Because its STAB moves are resisted by Dark-types, it is vulnerable to Pokemon like Scrafty, Skuntank, and Zoroark. Excellent partners to mitigate this include Florges, Paldean Tauros-F, and Pawmot, which can defeat Dark-types while benefiting from Hoopa's ability to break through threats like Galarian Slowbro, Palossand, and Houndstone.
What is the role of Mudsdale in the PU tier and what items can it use to support that role?

Mudsdale acts as a helpful wall in PU that checks physically offensive Pokemon like Paldean Tauros-F, Flamigo, Zoroark, Emboar, and Arcanine. It can use Leftovers to improve its longevity or a Rocky Helmet to punish physical attackers like Staraptor and Scyther. Its Stamina ability and access to utility moves like Stealth Rock and Roar further cement its effectiveness as a defensive wall.
What is the recommended defensive moveset and item for Jellicent in the PU tier?

The defensive Jellicent set typically utilizes Hex as its attacking move and either Will-O-Wisp or Toxic for status. Will-O-Wisp is preferred to catch physical attackers like Absol and Silvally-Ghost, while Toxic is useful for crippling bulkier checks like Lanturn. Colbur Berry is the optimal item to weaken Knock Off from opponents such as Sneasel and Gallade, though Heavy-Duty Boots is an alternative to provide immunity to entry hazards. This set uses 76 Speed EVs to outspeed Jolly Glastrier.
How does the Synchronize ability function during battle when a Pokémon is afflicted by an opponent?

Whenever a Pokémon with Synchronize is burned, paralyzed, or poisoned by another Pokémon, the attacking Pokémon that inflicted the ailment will also be afflicted with the same status condition. This includes passing on bad poison if the user is badly poisoned, but it does not apply to status ailments received from sources other than a direct attack, such as toxic spikes or a flame orb.
What is the effect of the ability Wimp Out?
Wimp Out is an ability that causes a Pokémon to switch out after being hit by a move that drops its HP to half or less.
What happens after you show the hatched Togepi to Professor Elm in Generation II?
After the player shows the hatched Togepi to Professor Elm, he will reward the player with an Everstone.
How does the Tunnel Mail item function in Generation IV games?

The Tunnel Mail allows players to write messages using a predefined list of words and phrases from the easy chat system. It is held by a Pokémon and can be traded to other games of the same generation. The message persists as long as the item is held or in the Mailbox, but cannot be stored in the Bag or Item Storage without being deleted. Pokémon holding it cannot enter the PC or Day Care, and it cannot be transferred to Generation V games.
What moveset and strategy make Aromatisse an effective late-game sweeper in the SM NU tier?

Aromatisse functions as a late-game sweeper by using Trick Room to offset its low Speed and Nasty Plot to boost its Special Attack. Its core moves include Moonblast as its primary STAB attack, while Psychic provides coverage against Poison-types. Additionally, it can run Hidden Power Fire to hit Steel-types like Klinklang and Silvally-Steel or Hidden Power Ground as an alternative. Its typing and bulk also allow it to check threats like Hariyama, Sneasel, and Malamar.
What effect does the Griseous Orb have when held by Giratina?
When held by Giratina, the Griseous Orb boosts the power of its Dragon-type and Ghost-type moves by 1.2 times their base power and causes the holder to be in its Origin Forme.
What are the benefits of using a Choice Scarf or Choice Band on Flamigo in NU competitive play?

Choice Scarf allows Flamigo to function as a revenge killer, enabling it to outspeed and defeat faster threats such as Inteleon, Kilowattrel, Tornadus, and Cinccino. Conversely, Choice Band turns Flamigo into a potent wallbreaker, granting it the power to 2HKO bulky defensive tanks like Galarian Slowbro, Vaporeon, and Diancie.
At what level does Croagunk evolve into Toxicroak?
Croagunk evolves into Toxicroak at level 37.
What happens when a Zebstrika becomes angry?
When a Zebstrika is angry, it fires lightning bolts from its mane. An indication that the Pokémon is angry is that its white mane illuminates in a yellow glow.
Are there currently any ORAS Monotype competitive strategy analyses available for Slurpuff?
No, there are no ORAS Monotype analyses for Slurpuff available at this time, as those are not currently being worked on.
How does the Fluffy ability affect the damage a Pokémon receives from different move types?

A Pokémon with the Fluffy ability takes half damage from moves that make contact. However, it takes double damage from Fire-type moves. If a Fire-type move makes contact, it deals regular damage, and moves affected by Long Reach that make contact also deal regular damage.
At what level does a Snorunt evolve into Glalie?
A Snorunt evolves into a Glalie at level 42.
What is the secondary effect of the Max Move Max Steelspike?
When used, Max Steelspike deals damage to the target and raises the Defense of both the user and its ally by one stage.
What is the recommended EV spread for Heliolisk in XY competitive play, and what item does it typically hold?

Heliolisk should use an EV spread of maximum Special Attack and maximum Speed to ensure it hits as hard and as fast as possible. It typically holds a Life Orb, which boosts the power of all its attacks, though this item can wear the Pokemon down quickly.
How does the Harvest ability work in terms of Berry recovery?

After each turn, if a Pokémon with Harvest has consumed a Berry and is not holding an item, it has a 50% chance of regaining that Berry. This probability increases to a 100% chance if the weather is strong sunlight.
What items are used to evolve the Pokémon Applin?
The items used to evolve Applin are the Tart Apple, Sweet Apple, and Syrupy Apple.
What is the effect of the Defeatist ability on a Pokémon's stats?
The Defeatist ability causes a Pokémon's Attack and Special Attack stats to be halved whenever its HP drops to half or less.
What is the effect of the Pokémon ability Poison Heal?

If a Pokémon with Poison Heal is poisoned, it will recover 1/8 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn instead of taking damage from the poison. This effect also applies if the Pokémon is suffering from bad poison.
What is the specific evolution method for a Gloom to evolve into a Bellossom?
Gloom evolves into Bellossom when it is exposed to a Sun Stone.
What is the catch rate multiplier for a Great Ball when used on a wild Pokémon?
When used in battle on a wild Pokémon, the Great Ball has a catch rate multiplier of 1.5×.
What are the base stats for the Pokémon Ditto?
Ditto has a base stat total where every individual stat—HP, Attack, Defense, Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Speed—is 48.
What is the primary effect of the move Disable and how long does it last?

Disable prevents a target from using its last used move for a random duration of 4 to 7 turns, or until the target leaves the field. The move fails if the target has not used a move since entering the field, if the last move used has 0 PP remaining, or if the target already has a move disabled.
What is the primary effect of the move Court Change?
Court Change is a Pokémon move that allows the user to swap the effects currently active on either side of the field.
What is the primary effect of the Pokémon move Vine Whip?
Vine Whip is a Pokémon move that inflicts regular damage upon the target by striking them with slender, whiplike vines.
What is the function of the Galactic Warehouse storage key?
The Galactic Warehouse key is an item used to access Team Galactic's warehouse located at the edge of Veilstone City.
What are the base stats of the Pokémon Stunfisk?
Stunfisk has the following base stats: HP 109, Attack 66, Defense 84, Sp. Atk 81, Sp. Def 99, and Speed 32.
What is the primary effect of the Pokémon move Trick Or Treat?
The Trick Or Treat move adds the Ghost type to the target's existing types.
What kind of damage does the move Precipice Blades inflict?
The move Precipice Blades inflicts regular damage by manifesting the power of the land in fearsome blades of stone to attack opposing Pokémon.
What is the primary function of the Fame Checker item in the Pokémon series?

The Fame Checker is a device used to record and store information about famous people, allowing the user to instantly recall details that they have previously heard or seen regarding these individuals.
What are the common checks and counters to Incineroar, and why does it struggle against them?

Incineroar struggles against faster wallbreakers like Basculegion, Breloom, and Heracross because of its low Speed stat. Additionally, it must be wary of opposing Knock Off users like Tsareena, Alolan Muk, and Wo-Chien, as it relies heavily on Heavy-Duty Boots. It can also be crippled by Chandelure using Trick, and its lack of reliable recovery makes it easy to wear down throughout a battle.
What is the primary effect of the Desolate Land ability when it is active in battle?

Desolate Land creates extremely harsh sunlight. This weather condition causes damage-dealing Water-type moves to fail, prevents other weather-modifying moves like Rain Dance or Sunny Day from working, and stops the activation of Abilities like Drizzle, Drought, Sand Stream, and Snow Warning.
What happens when a Pokémon uses the Fighting-type move Final Gambit?

Final Gambit causes the user to faint, and the target receives damage equal to the user's remaining HP before the move was used. If the move misses or is protected against, it fails and the user does not faint. If the move is used in a Max or Tera Raid Battle, it also fails without causing the user to faint.
What are the base stats and primary types of the Pokémon Pawmot?
Pawmot is an Electric and Fighting type Pokémon. Its base stats consist of 70 HP, 115 Attack, 70 Defense, 70 Special Attack, 60 Special Defense, and 105 Speed.
What are the base stats and primary types of the Paradox Pokémon Koraidon?
Koraidon is a Fighting and Dragon type Pokémon. Its base stats are: HP 100, Attack 135, Defense 115, Sp. Atk 85, Sp. Def 100, and Speed 135.
At what level does Foongus evolve into Amoonguss?
Foongus evolves into Amoonguss at level 39.
Which Pokemon act as checks to Emboar in the BW competitive tier?

In the BW competitive tier, Emboar is innately kept in check by Pokemon such as Slowking, Qwilfish, and Rhydon. Other common switch-ins that handle Emboar effectively include Poliwrath, Alomomola, Feraligatr, Kabutops, and Quagsire.
What effect does the Stench Ability have when a Pokémon with it uses a damaging move?

When a Pokémon with the Stench Ability uses a damaging move, it has a 10% chance of causing the target to flinch. This effect does not stack with King's Rock, Razor Fang, or moves that already possess a flinch chance. For multistrike moves, each individual hit has its own independent 10% chance to cause the target to flinch.
What is the primary effect of the Roto Exp Points item?
Roto Exp Points is a Rotom Power that slightly increases the amount of Experience Points your Pokémon receive after finishing a battle.
At what level does Rattata evolve into Raticate?
Rattata evolves into Raticate at level 20.
What is the primary function of the Linking Cord item in Pokémon?
The Linking Cord is a consumable item that triggers the evolution of specific Pokémon, which are all species that can alternatively evolve when traded.
What are the base stats for the Fighting-type Pokémon Hitmontop?
Hitmontop has the following base stats: HP 50, Attack 95, Defense 95, Sp. Atk 35, Sp. Def 110, and Speed 70.
What is the primary role of Lycanroc in competitive play, and what move is crucial for its late-game sweeping capability?

Lycanroc is primarily used as a late-game sweeper, utilizing its high Attack, high Speed, and powerful Z-move, Splintered Stormshards. To successfully sweep, Lycanroc relies on the Lycanium Z item and the Swords Dance move to help break through checks. It also utilizes priority moves like Accelerock and coverage options such as Brick Break or Fire Fang to clean up weakened teams.
What is the function of Gooey Mulch when used on a patch of soil?

Gooey Mulch is a plant fertilizer that increases the number of times new plants grow where mature plants have withered. Specifically, it allows plants to regrow 25% more times after dying. It is noted to have no effect on soil in the Hoenn region.
In which Pokémon generation was Chili Sauce introduced, and what is its primary use?
Chili Sauce was introduced in Generation IX. It serves as an ingredient that can be used to make a sandwich.
What is the effect of the ability Ripen when a Pokémon consumes a held Berry in battle?

The ability Ripen doubles the effects of held Berries when they are eaten by the Pokémon in battle, or when the Pokémon is targeted by a Flung Berry. This enhancement applies to Berries that restore HP or PP, reduce damage, raise stats (with the exception of the Lansat Berry), or deal damage to attackers.
What is the effect of using a Tough Candy Xl on a Pokémon?
When given to a Pokémon that is at level 60 or higher, a Tough Candy Xl will increase that Pokémon's Defense stat.
What is Coalossal's role in the ZU tier and what teammates are recommended to support it?

Coalossal serves as a Spikes setter and entry hazard remover with defensive utility. Due to its 4x weaknesses to Water and Ground, it should be paired with Grass-types like Tangela, which tanks hits from Fighting-types, and Thwackey, which provides passive recovery and reduces Earthquake damage. Knock Off absorbers like Uxie and Alolan Persian are also recommended to preserve Coalossal's Heavy-Duty Boots.
At what level does a Machop evolve into a Machoke?
A Machop evolves into a Machoke starting at level 28.
What is the recommended moveset and playstyle for a Choice Band Golurk?

Choice Band Golurk functions as a powerful wallbreaker. Its core moveset includes Poltergeist and Earthquake for STAB damage, and Close Combat to hit defensive Dark-types like Guzzlord, Scrafty, and Snorlax. The final move slot is flexible; Stone Edge is used to OHKO Talonflame and hit Flying-types like Mantine and Braviary, while Heat Crash hits itemless Grass-types such as Vileplume, Rotom-C, and Dhelmise.
What is the primary effect of holding a Yellow Scarf in a Pokémon Contest?
The Yellow Scarf is a hold item that increases the holder's Toughness aspect during a Pokémon Contest or a Super Contest's Visual Competition.
What is the evolution level required for Hakamo-o to evolve into Kommo-o?
Hakamo-o evolves into Kommo-o at level 45.
What happens when a Pokémon holds a Fairy Gem in battle?

When a Pokémon holds a Fairy Gem, the power of its first Fairy-type move used is increased by 30%, after which the item is consumed. If the Pokémon attempts to use the move Fling while holding a Fairy Gem, the move will fail.
What is the criteria for a Pokémon to be officially designated as OU in Smogon's competitive format?

A Pokémon is designated as OU in Smogon's format if it makes up at least approximately 4.52% of total weighted usage in the tier. This reflects a statistical likelihood where a typical competitive player is more than 50% likely to encounter that Pokémon at least once within 15 battles.
What is the primary effect of the Pokémon move Night Daze?
Night Daze inflicts regular damage on the target and has a chance to lower the target's accuracy by one stage.
What is the primary role of Mimikyu in the SS competitive tier and how does its ability help it?

Mimikyu functions as a premier setup sweeper due to its good Attack stat, strong STAB moves, and access to Swords Dance. Its viability is primarily driven by its ability, Disguise, which allows it to safely set up or revenge kill dangerous foes like Dragon Dance Flygon, Sharpedo, and Polteageist. Because of this, it serves as a valuable asset against hyper offense teams.
What is Victini's primary role and key move in the XY competitive tier?

Victini functions as a solid wallbreaker thanks to its access to V-create, which is one of the most powerful moves in the game. It features high Base Power and perfect accuracy when combined with the ability Victory Star, allowing Victini to overcome most counters.
What is a unique effect of the Pokémon move Storm Throw?
Storm Throw is a move that inflicts regular damage, and it has the unique characteristic that it always scores a critical hit against the target.
What are some of the ways the Spelon Berry can be used in the Pokémon games?

Spelon Berries cannot be used directly but serve as ingredients for Pokéblocks and Poffins. In Pokémon Emerald, they can be exchanged for an HP Up in the Lilycove Pokémon Center. In Diamond, Pearl, and Platinum, they can be traded for accessories at the Flower Shop. Additionally, in Generation V, they can be sold for $500 to the gourmet maniac on Route 5, and in the Pokémon Dream World, they were used to obtain Décor.
What happens to the user's stats after using the move Spin Out?
When Spin Out is used, it inflicts damage on the target and lowers the user's Speed stat by two stages.
What is the purpose of the Silver Pick item in Pokémon games?
The Silver Pick is a picnic item introduced in Generation IX that is used to complete a sandwich made during a picnic.
What are the base stats of the Paradox Pokémon Slither Wing?
Slither Wing has the following base stats: HP 85, Attack 135, Defense 79, Sp. Atk 85, Sp. Def 105, and Speed 81.
How does the Snow Cloak ability affect a Pokémon during a hailstorm?

During hail, a Pokémon with the Snow Cloak ability gains 1.25 times its evasion, and it is immune to hail damage regardless of its type. Additionally, the evasion bonus provided by this ability does not count as a stat modifier.
What is the recommended moveset and EV spread for a wallbreaker Conkeldurr in XY?

Conkeldurr should be used as a wallbreaker with the Guts ability and a Flame Orb to boost damage. Key moves include Drain Punch for longevity, Facade to hit resisting types, Knock Off for utility against Psychic- and Ghost-types, and Mach Punch for priority. The recommended EV spread is 252 Attack EVs with an Adamant nature for maximum damage, 136 Speed EVs to outspeed Clefable and Hippowdon, and the remainder in HP for bulk.
Under what condition does the move Steel Roller fail?
The move Steel Roller fails if the ground has not turned into a terrain.
What is unique about the Ability called Battery?
Charjabug is the only known Pokémon that can have Battery as an Ability.
What happens to the power of the move taught by TM98 if the user's previous move failed?
If the user’s previous move has failed, the power of the move taught by TM98 doubles.
What is Necrozma's role in the RU tier and what are its key moves for the Calm Mind sweeper set?

In RU, Necrozma acts as a premier wallbreaker. Its flagship Calm Mind sweeper set utilizes Photon Geyser as its primary STAB move to deal massive damage, Heat Wave to hit Steel-types like Metagross and Forretress, Calm Mind to boost its Special Attack and Special Defense, and Moonlight to maintain its health.
What is the effect of the Stamina ability in Pokémon?
The Stamina ability raises the Pokémon's Defense stat by one stage whenever the Pokémon takes damage from a move or is hit by an attack.
What roles does Florges fulfill in the tier and what are some of its primary defensive counters?

Florges serves as a defensive staple, a Wish user, and a cleric in the tier. It is effective at checking special attackers like Mega Blastoise, Noivern, and Mega Sceptile. However, its mediocre physical bulk makes it struggle against threats like Choice Band Bewear and Guts Machamp, and its passivity allows offensive threats like Metagross, Ninetales, and Arcanine to take advantage of it or use it as setup fodder.
What are some of the main checks and counters to Zamazenta in SV competitive play?

Zamazenta is checked by Pokemon that can punish its contact moves, such as Zapdos with Static, Moltres with Flame Body, and Hatterene, Corviknight, and Landorus-T with Rocky Helmet. It also struggles against specific defensive answers like Gliscor, Toxapex, Clefable, and Hatterene depending on its set. Additionally, bulky Ghost-types such as Pecharunt, Sinistcha, and Gholdengo hinder it, and many Pokemon can use Tera Ghost to improve their matchup against Zamazenta.
What is the purpose of the Pokémon item known as Data Card 06?
Data Card 06 is a Pokémon item used to record and show the number of times a trainer has come in first place in the Pokéathlon Relay Run.
What is the primary effect of the Sharp Beak held item in Pokémon?
The Sharp Beak is a held item that increases the power of the holder's Flying-type moves. In the generations it has appeared, this boost ranges from 10% to 20%.
At what level does Hoothoot evolve into Noctowl, and what are its primary types?
Hoothoot evolves into Noctowl at level 20. It is a dual-type Normal and Flying Pokémon.
What is the evolution chain for Aurorus, and at what level does it evolve?
Aurorus is part of an evolution chain that begins with Amaura, which evolves into Aurorus at level 39.
How does the Swarm ability affect Bug-type moves when a Pokémon's HP is low?

When a Pokémon with the Swarm ability has less than or equal to 1/3 of its maximum HP remaining, it boosts the power of Bug-type moves by 50%. This is achieved by increasing the Pokémon's Attack or Special Attack by 50% during damage calculation when using a Bug-type move.
What is the primary role of Crabominable in competitive play, and what items or team structures support it?

Crabominable serves as a powerful physical wallbreaker due to its high Attack and Iron Fist ability. It functions effectively as an Assault Vest user to leverage its special bulk and Ice resistance, or as a hard-hitting attacker on Trick Room teams to mitigate its low Speed. Its EV spread typically maximizes HP and special bulk, supported by an Adamant nature to maintain significant damage output.
What is a recommended competitive moveset and build for Araquanid to serve as a Sticky Web setter?

To function as a Sticky Web setter, Araquanid should use the moves Liquidation, Magic Coat, and Mirror Coat. It should hold the Splash Plate to boost its offensive presence, paired with the Water Bubble ability. An Adamant nature with maximum Attack investment maximizes damage output, while maximum Speed investment allows it to outspeed defensive Pokemon like Porygon2 and Vaporeon.
What is the evolution chain and the required level for Gumshoos?
Gumshoos evolves from Yungoos, and the evolution occurs at level 20.
What effect does the Eviolite item have on a Pokémon that is not fully evolved?

The Eviolite boosts both the Defense and Special Defense of a Pokémon holding it by 50%, provided that the holder is not fully evolved and possesses a non-zero value for an evolution method in its internal personal data.
At what level does a Nidoran♀ evolve into Nidorina?
Nidoran♀ evolves into Nidorina at level 16.
How does the move Lock On affect the accuracy of the user's next attack?

Lock On ensures that the user's next move is guaranteed to hit the target, even ignoring accuracy and evasion modifiers. Additionally, it allows the user to hit Pokémon that are off the field due to moves like dig or fly, and it allows one-hit KO moves to hit if the user is of an equal or higher level than the target.
Why does Minior struggle to compete as a setup sweeper compared to Pokémon like Comfey or Braviary?

Minior is considered less reliable than other sweepers due to its lackluster stats in Meteor forme and poor defensive typing, which make it difficult for the Pokémon to force opponents out to safely set up. Additionally, it struggles to beat bulky Water- and Ground-types like Vaporeon, Steelix, and Rhydon even after a Shell Smash boost, and it lacks significant early- or mid-game presence because its health must be preserved to attempt a sweep.
What happens when a Pokémon uses the move Max Hailstorm?
Max Hailstorm is an Ice-type attack used by Dynamax Pokémon that inflicts regular damage and summons a hailstorm that lasts for five turns.
What is the primary role of Aurorus on hail hyper offense teams and what item is recommended to ensure its success?

Aurorus serves as a dedicated Stealth Rock lead on hail hyper offense teams. It is recommended to equip it with a Focus Sash to guarantee that it can successfully set up Stealth Rock against the majority of the metagame despite its poor defensive typing and low speed.
At what level does Rattata evolve into Raticate, and what are its base stats?
Rattata evolves into Raticate at level 20. Its base stats are: HP 55, Attack 81, Defense 60, Sp. Atk 50, Sp. Def 70, and Speed 97.
What is the recommended moveset and playstyle for Alomomola in the SM metagame?

Alomomola is used as a premier Wish passer on stall and balanced teams, utilizing Wish for recovery and team support, Protect for scouting and self-healing, Toxic for stalling, and Scald to burn physical attackers. Knock Off is an alternative to remove items like Black Sludge or Leftovers. It relies on its high HP and Regenerator ability to pivot, though it is vulnerable to special attackers, entry hazards, and powerful wallbreakers like Terrakion and Crawdaunt.
What is Krookodile's role in the UnderUsed metagame and what are some of its primary moves?

Krookodile is a threat in the UnderUsed metagame, functioning as a Stealth Rock setter or a Choice Scarf revenge killer. Its primary moves include Knock Off, which removes opponent items; Earthquake, used to revenge kill foes like Terrakion and Infernape; Pursuit, used for trapping switching foes like Gengar and Latias; and Stone Edge, which covers Flying-types like Togekiss and Moltres. Crunch is also an option for reliable revenge killing against targets like Latias.
How does the Tera Shell ability affect damage-dealing moves when a Pokémon is at full HP?
When a Pokémon with the Tera Shell ability is at full HP, all damage-dealing moves that hit it will be considered not very effective.
What is the primary role of Lurantis in competitive play, and which moves allow it to take advantage of its Contrary ability?

Lurantis acts as a utility check for entry hazard setters like Rhydon, Stunfisk, and Qwilfish, utilizing Defog to clear hazards and Synthesis to maintain health. It leverages its Contrary ability with Leaf Storm, which boosts Special Attack by two stages per use, and Superpower, which provides Fighting-type coverage and neutral damage against various types while also providing stat boosts.
What is the optimal item choice for Emboar as a wallbreaker in SV competitive play?

Heavy-Duty Boots is the preferred item for Emboar to increase its longevity and provide flexibility, though a Choice Band can be utilized if the player wants to maximize its wallbreaking power, allowing it to 2HKO bulkier Pokemon like Mudsdale and Palossand with Flare Blitz.
What are the base stats and primary type of the Pokémon Orthworm?
Orthworm is a Steel-type Pokémon with the following base stats: 70 HP, 85 Attack, 145 Defense, 60 Sp. Atk, 55 Sp. Def, and 65 Speed.
What is the purpose of the Lost Item in the Pokémon games?
The Lost Item is a Pokémon item that can be traded to the Copycat in exchange for a pass. It is described as the Mime Jr. doll or the Poké Doll that the Copycat lost.
What items are commonly used on Virizion to improve its performance in battle, and what roles do they serve?

Life Orb is used to compensate for Virizion's poor unboosted attack power, while Lum Berry allows Virizion to safely set up against Pokemon like Mudsdale or Vaporeon by protecting it from status moves like Toxic or Scald burns.
What are the base stats and primary ability of the Fairy-type Pokémon Alcremie?
Alcremie has the following base stats: HP 65, Attack 60, Defense 75, Sp. Atk 110, Sp. Def 121, and Speed 64. Its primary ability is Sweet Veil, while its hidden ability is Aroma Veil.
What is the strategic role of Iron Boulder on hyper offense teams and which specific Tera types can be used to improve its matchups?

Iron Boulder acts as a setup sweeper and speed control, outspeeding common threats like Iron Valiant and Iron Moth. Tera Flying allows it to beat Gliscor; Tera Fighting boosts Close Combat to break through Skarmory and Ting-Lu while resisting Sucker Punch; and Tera Ground boosts Earthquake to secure OHKOs on bulky Gholdengo and avoid Raging Bolt's Thunderclap.
What are the base stats and primary ability of the Pokémon Crocalor?
Crocalor has a base HP of 81, Attack of 55, Defense of 78, Sp. Atk of 90, Sp. Def of 58, and Speed of 49. Its primary ability is Blaze.
What is the purpose of the Alakazite item?
Alakazite is a mysterious Mega Stone that, when held by Alakazam, enables it to Mega Evolve into Mega Alakazam during battle.
What is the effect of holding a Kebia Berry in battle?
When a Pokémon holding a Kebia Berry would take super-effective damage from a Poison-type attack, the berry is consumed to halve the damage taken.
How does Haxorus's ability Mold Breaker benefit it in the UU metagame?

Mold Breaker allows Haxorus to act as a stallbreaker by bypassing the abilities of the tier's Unaware users, such as Quagsire and Pyukumuku, which are typically used to answer physical setup sweepers. Additionally, it provides an edge over other setup sweepers like Cobalion and allows Haxorus to deal heavy damage to Steel-types like Bronzong.
What move is taught by using TM67?
TM67 is a Pokémon item that teaches the move Recycle to a compatible Pokémon. When used, it allows the user to recycle a single-use item that has already been used in battle so it can be used again.
What is the function of the Tiny Mushroom in Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen?

In Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen, the Tiny Mushroom can be used as payment for the Move Reminder on Two Island. By giving the Move Reminder two Tiny Mushrooms, a player can have one of their Pokémon relearn a move.
What are some of the utility and attacking moves used on Hydreigon in SM UU?

Hydreigon utilizes Draco Meteor as its most powerful STAB attack, Dark Pulse for reliable coverage, and U-turn for safe pivoting. Fire Blast is used to target Steel-types like Cobalion, Scizor, and Klefki, while Defog allows it to function as an offensive entry hazard remover.
What happens when a Pokémon with the Snow Warning ability enters a battle?
When a Pokémon with the Snow Warning ability enters a battle, it summons a hailstorm. The weather changes to hail and will continue until it is cancelled by another weather condition.
What is the function of the Smart Candy Xl item when used on a Pokémon?
Smart Candy Xl is a large candy packed with energy. When given to a Pokémon that is level 60 or higher, it functions to increase that Pokémon’s Sp. Atk stat.
What happens when a Pokémon uses the move Pluck on a target holding a berry?

When Pluck is used on a target holding a berry, the move inflicts regular damage, and the user takes and consumes the berry to gain its effect. If the target is holding a berry, the move's power is doubled. Note that if the target is holding a jaboca or rowap berry, the berry is removed but provides no effect to the user.
How does the Simple ability affect a Pokémon's stat stages?

The Simple ability doubles the effectiveness of stat stages. When a Pokémon with Simple uses a move that raises or lowers a stat, the number of stages is doubled, meaning a one-stage change effectively acts as a two-stage change. However, the total effect of these stat stages cannot exceed the usual game caps.
What items can Toxapex use to enhance its role as a mixed wall, and how do they benefit its performance?

Toxapex can use Heavy-Duty Boots to increase its longevity, Rocky Helmet to punish physical pivots like Scizor and Lokix, or Leftovers for passive recovery that stacks with Regenerator to reduce the need for Recover.
What is the primary purpose of a Comet Shard in Pokémon?
A Comet Shard is a Pokémon item that can be sold to shops for a high price, often described as a shard that fell to the ground when a comet approached.
At what level does Servine evolve into Serperior?
Servine evolves into Serperior at level 36.
What happens to a Pokémon after using Raging Fury for two to three turns?
After using Raging Fury for two to three turns, the user becomes confused due to fatigue.
What is the effect of the Curious Medicine ability?
When a Pokémon with the Curious Medicine ability enters a battle, it scatters medicine from its shell to remove all stat changes from its allies.
How does the Chill Drive affect Genesect's Techno Blast move?
If a Genesect is holding the Chill Drive, its Techno Blast move becomes an Ice-type attack. Additionally, holding the item changes the Genesect to its Chill Drive form.
What is the effect of using an X Attack on a Pokémon during a battle?
Using an X Attack on a party Pokémon in battle raises the target's Attack stat by one stage. This boost lasts for the duration of the battle, but the effect wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.
What happens when Shroomish senses danger?

When Shroomish senses danger, it shakes its body and scatters toxic spores from the top of its head. These spores are capable of making trees and weeds wilt, and they can cause whole-body pain if inhaled by others.
What are the base stats for the Pokémon Castform?
Castform has a base stat total of 70 for each of its attributes, including HP, Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense, and Speed.
At what level does Combee evolve into Vespiquen, and what are Vespiquen's primary types?
Combee evolves into Vespiquen at level 21. Vespiquen is a Bug and Flying type Pokémon.
How does the Lucky Egg item affect a Pokémon's experience gain when held?

When a Pokémon holds the Lucky Egg, it gains 150% of the experience points it would otherwise earn. This effect stacks with other experience-increasing factors, such as the natural bonus provided to Pokémon with a different Original Trainer (OT).
How does Morpeko's Hunger Switch Ability affect its signature move, Aura Wheel?

Morpeko's Hunger Switch Ability causes its signature move, Aura Wheel, to change types based on its current form. In Full Belly Mode, Aura Wheel is an Electric-type move. In Hangry Mode, which Morpeko enters at the end of each turn, Aura Wheel becomes a Dark-type move.
What is the recommended moveset and EV spread for a competitive Galvantula?

Galvantula should run Sticky Web, Thunder, Bug Buzz, and Volt Switch. The recommended EV spread is maximum Special Attack and Speed with a Timid nature to outspeed base 100 Speed Pokemon like Flygon. It should hold a Life Orb for a 1.3x damage boost and use the Compound Eyes ability to increase Thunder's accuracy to 91%.
What is the origin of the Relic Crown?
The Relic Crown is a heavy crown created by an ancient civilization approximately 3,000 years ago.
What move is taught by TM66, and what happens if the user moves after the target when using it?
TM66 teaches the move Payback. If the user moves after the target, the power of this attack is doubled.
How is damage calculated when a Pokémon uses the move Beat Up?

The damage calculation for Beat Up uses the user's Attack stat alone, allowing it to be affected by boosts like Swords Dance and items like Choice Band. The base power of each strike is now individually based on the Attack base stats of the Pokémon in the user's party.
What are some of the physical walls that can reliably check or counter Furfrou in competitive play?
Furfrou can be checked or countered by physical walls like Mawile, Gourgeist-XL, and Dusclops, as these Pokémon can easily tank or play around its attacks due to Furfrou's limited coverage options.
What are the base stats for the Pokémon Steelix?
The base stats for Steelix are as follows: HP 75, Attack 85, Defense 200, Sp. Atk 55, Sp. Def 65, and Speed 30.
What is the catch rate modifier of a Sport Ball when used in a wild encounter?
When used in a wild encounter, the Sport Ball attempts to catch a wild Pokémon with a catch rate modifier of 1.5×.
What is the purpose of a Psychic Tera Shard and how many are needed to change a Pokémon's Tera Type?

A Psychic Tera Shard is an item used to change a Pokémon's Tera Type to Psychic. You need to collect 50 Psychic Tera Shards and take them to the Treasure Eatery in Medali to perform this change, though it cannot be used on Ogerpon or Terapagos.
At what level does Nickit evolve into Thievul, and what are its possible abilities?
Nickit evolves into Thievul at level 18. Its available abilities are Run Away and Unburden, with Stakeout as its hidden ability.
What is the effect of the Rain Dish ability when it is raining?
When it is raining, a Pokémon with the Rain Dish ability will regain 1/16 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn.
What items and moves allow Excadrill to bypass common checks like Landorus-T and Gliscor?
Excadrill can use the combination of Swords Dance and Steelium Z to break through checks. Specifically, a +1 Corkscrew Crash can defeat Landorus-T, while a +2 Corkscrew Crash can overcome Gliscor.
At what level does Gastly evolve into Haunter?
Gastly evolves into Haunter at level 25.
What are the primary effects of the move Defog when used in battle?

Defog lowers the target's evasion by one stage and clears away fog. Additionally, it removes various barriers and entry hazards from the target's side of the field, including mist, light screen, reflect, safeguard, spikes, stealth rock, and toxic spikes.
What items and roles are commonly used for Volcanion in competitive play?

Volcanion functions as a hard-hitting wallbreaker. Its common items include Choice Specs, which allows it to deal massive damage to foes like Tornadus-T and offensive Garchomp, and Heavy-Duty Boots, which helps mitigate its weakness to Stealth Rock. When using Heavy-Duty Boots, players often use Toxic to wear down switch-ins like Gastrodon or Substitute to exploit forced switches from Pokemon like Ferrothorn and Heatran.
What kind of damage does the move Glacial Lance inflict?
The move Glacial Lance inflicts regular damage.
What effect does the Filter ability have when a Pokémon is hit by a super effective move?
The Filter ability reduces the damage taken from moves that are super effective against the Pokémon, causing it to take only 0.75 times the normal damage.
What can you receive in exchange for the Scanner item from Captain Stern?
You can trade the Scanner to Captain Stern for either a deep sea tooth or a deep sea scale.
What type of damage does the move Vice Grip inflict?
Vice Grip inflicts regular damage on the target by gripping and squeezing them with powerful pincers.
What happens to Ogerpon's stats when it uses the Embody Aspect ability upon Terastallizing?

When Ogerpon Terastallizes, its ability changes to Embody Aspect, which causes one of its stats to be raised by one stage. The specific stat that is increased depends on which mask Ogerpon is wearing.
At what level does Bronzor evolve into Bronzong?
Bronzor evolves into Bronzong at level 33.
What happens if a Pokémon that is not a Poison-type holds the Black Sludge item?
If a Pokémon that is not a Poison-type holds the Black Sludge, it will be damaged by 1/16 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn.
What is the primary effect of the Pokémon move Eerie Impulse?
The move Eerie Impulse functions by harshly lowering the target's Special Attack stat by two stages.
What are the base stats and primary typing of the Legendary Pokémon Ting-Lu?
Ting-Lu is a Dark/Ground type Pokémon with the following base stats: 155 HP, 110 Attack, 125 Defense, 55 Sp. Atk, 80 Sp. Def, and 45 Speed.
What is the primary item choice for Zebstrika in SM ZU competitive play, and why is it preferred?

Life Orb is the preferred item for Zebstrika because it provides the necessary power boost to secure OHKOs on Pokemon like Gourgeist-XL and Mareanie. Additionally, it offers the flexibility to switch moves, allowing Zebstrika to effectively target Grass- and Ground-type switch-ins that would otherwise resist its STAB Electric attacks.
What is the primary effect of using a Golden Razz Berry on a Pokémon?
The Golden Razz Berry is a Pokémon item that, when given to a Pokémon, makes it significantly easier to catch.
What is the primary function of the Venusaurite item?

The Venusaurite is a Mega Stone that allows Venusaur to Mega Evolve into Mega Venusaur, provided the Trainer has a Key Stone. It also enables Mega Evolution for Venusaur in Pokémon Shuffle and allows for Mega Evolution during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular Talent Round.
What are the effects of the move Sludge Wave?

Sludge Wave inflicts regular damage and has a percentage chance to poison the target, depending on its effect chance. The move functions by swamping the area around the user with a giant sludge wave, striking everything in the vicinity and potentially poisoning those it hits.
What is the primary function of the Steelixite held item in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire?

The Steelixite is a Mega Stone that, when held by a Steelix, allows it to Mega Evolve into Mega Steelix during battle provided the Trainer also has a Key Stone. It also enables Steelix to Mega Evolve during a Pokémon Contest Spectacular Talent round if the Trainer has a Key Stone.
How can Terrakion function effectively as a wallbreaker in competitive play?

Terrakion functions as a potent wallbreaker due to its high Attack stat and almost unresisted STAB combination, particularly when utilizing a Choice Band. It can use Earthquake to handle Jirachi or clean up weakened targets, Quick Attack for priority against faster foes like Cyclizar, and Tera Rock to boost Stone Edge for consistent 2HKOs on bulky targets like Slowbro. Additionally, it can use Swords Dance to break past walls like Chesnaught and Hippowdon.
At what level does a Pupitar evolve into Tyranitar?
A Pupitar evolves into a Tyranitar when it reaches level 55.
What is the primary function of the Blue Card item in the game?
The Blue Card is used to record points earned for answering the daily question on Buena’s radio show, also known as the Buena’s Password show.
What is the HP base stat of Alomomola and what are its available abilities?
Alomomola has a base HP stat of 165. Its available abilities are Healer and Hydration, and its hidden ability is Regenerator.
What is the primary function of the item Venusaurite?
Venusaurite is a type of Mega Stone that, when held by Venusaur, allows the Pokémon to Mega Evolve into Mega Venusaur during battle.
What kind of damage does the move Subzero Slammer Physical inflict?

Subzero Slammer Physical inflicts regular damage by using the user's Z-Power to dramatically drop the temperature and freeze the target with full force. Its power varies based on the original move used, and it is a move that cannot be used or remembered once forgotten.
What makes Garbodor a viable Spiker in the NU tier and what are some of its primary defensive roles?

Garbodor is a consistent Spiker in NU due to its bulk and Mono-Poison typing, which provides resistances to Bug, Poison, Grass, and Fighting. These resistances allow it to check threats such as Scyther, Skuntank, Hariyama, Hitmonchan, Lilligant, and Primeape. Additionally, its Aftermath ability weakens physical attackers upon its defeat, and it can utilize Rocky Helmet to chip away at the opponent's health.
What is the effect of the Roto Bargain item in Pokémon?
Roto Bargain is one of the Rotom Powers that serves to reduce the prices of products at Poké Marts by half.
What effect does the Lansat Berry have when held by a Pokémon during battle?
When a Pokémon holds a Lansat Berry and its HP falls to 1/4 of its maximum or less, the berry is consumed to raise the holder's critical hit chance by one stage.
How does the Gluttony ability change the way a Pokémon uses held Berries?
The Gluttony ability causes a Pokémon to eat its held Berry when its HP drops to 50% or less, which is earlier than the Berry's usual trigger threshold.
What is the secondary effect of the Max Move Max Quake?
Max Quake inflicts damage and increases the Special Defense of the user and its ally by one stage.
What happens when a Pokémon uses the move Thief and what are the limitations of this effect?

Thief inflicts regular damage and allows the user to permanently take the target's held item if the user is not currently holding one. This effect does not work if the user already has an item, or if the target has the ability Sticky Hold or Multitype. Additionally, targets cannot recover stolen items using Recycle, and the move cannot be selected by Assist or Metronome.
What is the effect of the Bug Gem when held by a Pokémon?
When held by a Pokémon, the Bug Gem increases the power of the first Bug-type move used by either 50% or 30%, depending on the game mechanics, and the item is consumed after the move is used.
What is the primary purpose of the Mixed Mushrooms item?
Mixed Mushrooms are an item that can be used as an ingredient for cooking at your camp.
What is a unique property of the Pokémon move Sunsteel Strike?

Sunsteel Strike inflicts regular damage and prevents other Pokémon's abilities from activating in response to it. It is capable of hitting through an opponent's disguise and can be used on the target regardless of their ability.
What is the primary effect of the Simple Pokémon ability on stat modifiers?

The Simple ability doubles the effect of any stat changes received by the Pokémon. Each stage of a stat modifier acts as two stages, though the final total remains capped at the standard minimum of -6 and maximum of 6.
How can a Trainer evolve their Panpour into a Simipour?
A Panpour can be evolved into a Simipour when it is exposed to a Water Stone.
At what levels do Rookidee and Corvisquire evolve?
Rookidee evolves into Corvisquire starting at level 18, and Corvisquire evolves into Corviknight starting at level 38.
What are the primary strengths and weaknesses of Reuniclus in the BW competitive tier?

Reuniclus is a top-tier Pokemon due to its Magic Guard ability, which ignores passive damage from hazards, status, and sandstorm. It boasts strong offensive coverage with moves like Focus Blast, Hidden Power Ice, and Thunder, along with set versatility including Calm Mind, 3 attacks, and Trick Room. However, its low Speed and lack of resistances leave it vulnerable to powerful attackers like Keldeo, Excadrill, and Latios, and it is easily punished by Pursuit users like Tyranitar and Scizor.
Why is the Tail Glow set preferred over the Z-Hypnosis set for Xurkitree?

The Tail Glow set is preferred because the Z-Hypnosis set is rarely effective at sweeping, even with Sticky Web support. Choice Scarf Landorus-T can ignore Sticky Web to revenge kill a +1 Xurkitree, and the Z-Hypnosis set is overly reliant on the move Hypnosis successfully landing to create a setup opportunity.
What are the secondary effects of the move Zing Zap?
Zing Zap is a move that inflicts regular damage. It also has a chance to make the target flinch due to the powerful electric shock delivered when the user crashes into the target.
What items and moves make Sandaconda an effective defensive threat in the SV ZU tier?

Sandaconda serves as a defensive wall and support Pokemon in SV ZU. It utilizes Rest paired with the Shed Skin ability for recovery, though it can be inconsistent. Key moves include Stealth Rock for hazard support, Glare for paralysis, Rock Blast to hit Flying-types like Articuno and Charizard, and Earthquake. Rocky Helmet is a recommended item to wear down physical attackers like Snorlax and Regirock and punish U-turn users.
What happens when a Pokemon uses the move Steam Eruption?
Steam Eruption inflicts regular damage on the target and has a chance to leave them with a burn.
What is the function of the stick that Braixen keeps in its tail?

Braixen keeps a stick in its tail which it sets alight using friction from its bushy tail fur. This stick serves to calm Braixen down, and the flame produced by the lit twig is used for both communication and attack.
At what level does Dartrix evolve into Decidueye, and what are Decidueye's primary types?
Dartrix evolves into Decidueye at level 34. Decidueye is a dual-type Pokémon with the Grass and Ghost types.
How does the Battle Bond ability affect the move Water Shuriken when activated?
When Battle Bond is activated, Water Shuriken's power increases from 15 to 20, and the move is guaranteed to hit the target three times.
At what level does Blitzle evolve into Zebstrika, and what are its base Speed and Attack stats?
Blitzle evolves into Zebstrika at level 27. It has a base Attack stat of 60 and a base Speed stat of 76.
How does the Sand Force ability affect moves during a sandstorm?

During a sandstorm, the Sand Force ability increases the base power of the Pokémon's rock-, ground-, and steel-type moves by 1.3 times. Additionally, a Pokémon with this ability does not take any damage from the sandstorm itself, regardless of its type.
What kind of damage does the move Searing Shot inflict, and what potential secondary effect does it have?
Searing Shot inflicts regular damage and has a percentage chance to burn the target.
What is the maximum number of coins a Coin Case can hold in the Generation IV games?
In Generation IV games, the Coin Case can hold a maximum of 50,000 coins.
What is the primary effect of the move taught by TM92?
TM92 teaches the move Trick Room, which creates a bizarre space for five turns in which slower Pokémon are able to move first.
How does the move Mat Block function in battle?
